title: Missing iptables rules for published ports when running with --net
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

When running a container connected to a non-default network and attempting to publish exposed ports, the appropriate iptables rules are not set up and the ports are inaccessible.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman network create podman-two`

2. `podman run -d -p 80:80 --net podman-two docker.io/library/nginx`

3. `iptables-save`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
# Generated by iptables-save v1.6.1 on Wed Oct 16 23:16:19 2019
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [33:2292]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [18:1696]
:CNI-ADMIN - [0:0]
:CNI-FORWARD - [0:0]
-A FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin rules" -j CNI-FORWARD
-A CNI-FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin rules" -j CNI-ADMIN
-A CNI-FORWARD -d 10.89.0.3/32 -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A CNI-FORWARD -s 10.89.0.3/32 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
# Completed on Wed Oct 16 23:16:19 2019
# Generated by iptables-save v1.6.1 on Wed Oct 16 23:16:19 2019
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [0:0]
:INPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [0:0]
:CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT - [0:0]
:CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ - [0:0]
:CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK - [0:0]
:CNI-c6d06d0f87c831d86afd3e0f - [0:0]
-A PREROUTING -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT
-A OUTPUT -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT
-A POSTROUTING -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd requiring masquerade" -j CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ
-A POSTROUTING -s 10.89.0.3/32 -m comment --comment "name: \"podman-two\" id: \"4d9e168d1667eac430fb9ab275d8f5cb2edcd63ef4a6d9abf643432e886bbcc5\"" -j CNI-c6d06d0f87c831d86afd3e0f
-A CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ -m mark --mark 0x2000/0x2000 -j MASQUERADE
-A CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd masquerade mark" -j MARK --set-xmark 0x2000/0x2000
-A CNI-c6d06d0f87c831d86afd3e0f -d 10.89.0.0/24 -m comment --comment "name: \"podman-two\" id: \"4d9e168d1667eac430fb9ab275d8f5cb2edcd63ef4a6d9abf643432e886bbcc5\"" -j ACCEPT
-A CNI-c6d06d0f87c831d86afd3e0f ! -d 224.0.0.0/4 -m comment --comment "name: \"podman-two\" id: \"4d9e168d1667eac430fb9ab275d8f5cb2edcd63ef4a6d9abf643432e886bbcc5\"" -j MASQUERADE
COMMIT
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
A CNI-DN-... chain with proper rules for port 80, as seen in this example (using --net podman):
```
# Generated by iptables-save v1.6.1 on Wed Oct 16 23:14:59 2019
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [54:3888]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [33:2972]
:CNI-ADMIN - [0:0]
:CNI-FORWARD - [0:0]
-A FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin rules" -j CNI-FORWARD
-A CNI-FORWARD -m comment --comment "CNI firewall plugin rules" -j CNI-ADMIN
-A CNI-FORWARD -d 10.88.0.6/32 -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A CNI-FORWARD -s 10.88.0.6/32 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
# Completed on Wed Oct 16 23:14:59 2019
# Generated by iptables-save v1.6.1 on Wed Oct 16 23:14:59 2019
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [1:52]
:INPUT ACCEPT [1:52]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [0:0]
:CNI-4be0e99205a904429375bd9b - [0:0]
:CNI-DN-4be0e99205a904429375b - [0:0]
:CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT - [0:0]
:CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ - [0:0]
:CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK - [0:0]
-A PREROUTING -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT
-A OUTPUT -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL -j CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT
-A POSTROUTING -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd requiring masquerade" -j CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ
-A POSTROUTING -s 10.88.0.6/32 -m comment --comment "name: \"podman\" id: \"0b3d861849bb6a3caf93487aefc429bcd754af4dc687135978f4686659579d65\"" -j CNI-4be0e99205a904429375bd9b
-A CNI-4be0e99205a904429375bd9b -d 10.88.0.0/16 -m comment --comment "name: \"podman\" id: \"0b3d861849bb6a3caf93487aefc429bcd754af4dc687135978f4686659579d65\"" -j ACCEPT
-A CNI-4be0e99205a904429375bd9b ! -d 224.0.0.0/4 -m comment --comment "name: \"podman\" id: \"0b3d861849bb6a3caf93487aefc429bcd754af4dc687135978f4686659579d65\"" -j MASQUERADE
-A CNI-DN-4be0e99205a904429375b -s 10.88.0.6/32 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK
-A CNI-DN-4be0e99205a904429375b -s 127.0.0.1/32 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK
-A CNI-DN-4be0e99205a904429375b -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j DNAT --to-destination 10.88.0.6:80
-A CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT -p tcp -m comment --comment "dnat name: \"podman\" id: \"0b3d861849bb6a3caf93487aefc429bcd754af4dc687135978f4686659579d65\"" -m multiport --dports 80 -j CNI-DN-4be0e99205a904429375b
-A CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ -m mark --mark 0x2000/0x2000 -j MASQUERADE
-A CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK -m comment --comment "CNI portfwd masquerade mark" -j MARK --set-xmark 0x2000/0x2000
COMMIT
# Completed on Wed Oct 16 23:14:59 2019
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

There do not appear to be any significant configuration differences for the newly created network:
```
root@sandbox:~# diff -w /etc/cni/net.d/*.conflist
3c3
<     "name": "podman",
---
>    "name": "podman-two",
7c7
<             "bridge": "cni-podman0",
---
>          "bridge": "cni-podman1",
20,21c20,21
< 			    "subnet": "10.88.0.0/16",
< 			    "gateway": "10.88.0.1"
---
>                      "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
>                      "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.6.1
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.2
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.1, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "19.04"
  MemFree: 420487168
  MemTotal: 1030766592
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: sandbox
  kernel: 5.0.0-32-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 4h 1m 52.89s (Approximately 0.17 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search: null
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/disco,now 1.6.1-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa3 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
DigitalOcean droplet, but occured on a bare-metal Arch Linux install as well. Podman was installed from `ppa:projectatomic/ppa`.

comments.body: Can you post the CNI conflist the network created? It should be in `/etc/cni/net.d/` and is probably named something like `podman-two.conflist`?
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

I'm having the exact same problem as the OP, but even with the default network.
Any started container which exposes a port is only reachable directly from the host where podman is running.

The only lines that get added to the output of "iptables --list" are those two when I start nginx on exposed port 8080:

ACCEPT     all  --  anywhere             10.88.0.29           ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED
ACCEPT     all  --  10.88.0.29           anywhere

(In chain CNI-FORWARD)

I'm on fedora atomic host 29.

Let me know if more information from my system is needed to dig into this.

Also running into this with a fresh checkout of podman (Debian buster):
```
# podman version
Version:            1.6.4-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.6
Git Commit:         db32ed1ae8374aac9c4058607ad9cf294505c952
Built:              Sun Nov 17 18:43:30 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Closing as this appears to be fixed
